column7: Objective
column9: RewardDesign
column11: ImplementationNotes

column6: New game begins in a damaged survivor pod.
column7: Exit pod, recover emergency cord kit, stabilize bleeding, and reach daylight.
column8: Fixed: PlayerStart crash pod inside StartSafeRadiusMeters.
column9: Tutorial authority: unlock basic interaction, body HUD, and emergency inventory. No external reward.
column11: Teaches immediate survival, not quest UI dependency.

column7: Find or construct a windbreak, place first camp marker, and cache one safe ration stack.
column8: Near PlayerStart; use safe-start biome whitelist.
column9: BaseClaimToken, camp marker, +5 MG because survivors see a rally point.
column10: Night without shelter adds morale shock.
column11: The reward is the player's own established camp, not an NPC gift.

column7: Locate the first survivor signal, recover 1-3 survivors, and escort them to camp.
column8: POI placeholder: SurvivorPod or CryoPodCluster within start band.
column9: Population unlock; first potential leader candidate; survivors bring personal inventory only.
column10: Survivors can die if ignored for too many days.
column11: Sets up leadership as people, not menu classes.

column7: Secure any two water sources: boiled crash water, filtered basin water, or stored ration water.
column8: GalleyWreck, MeltlineEdge, or crash tank placeholder.
column9: Water routine unlocked; +MR to all recovered survivors; Research learns WaterSafety_Pending.
column11: Avoids random tech reward; unlock is procedural knowledge.

column6: Dusk approaching or first day complete.
column7: Light camp, assign rest, place emergency food/water, and survive one night.
column9: Unlock daily colony report and first leader complaint system.
column10: Night predator/raid risk if noise high.
column11: First explicit introduction of daily diagnostics.

column7: Build Log Yard, Stone Pile, Pantry, and Scrap Heap; deposit correct items into correct depots.
column9: Station pull logic enabled; +10 MR for workers due readable supply flow.
column10: Crafting stalls if depots not built.
column11: Teaches physical logistics law.

column7: Build ST_CAMP_WORKBENCH and craft first repair kit.
column9: Repair loop unlocked; leader issue state can report maintenance debt.
column11: Camp Workbench remains T0 fail-safe.

column7: Assign at least three roles and appoint a temporary leader for one department.
column9: Leadership XP begins; leader panel unlocked.
column11: Any NPC can be leader; this starts weak by design.

column6: Player obtains native food or meat.
column7: Classify first food source or rely on sealed Earth food until sample is researched.
column9: CodexDiscoveryState and EdibilityState UI unlock.
column11: Reinforces EarthSealed safe floor.

column7: Recover one REF_BASIC_TOOLING reference component from a tool-gated wreck.
column9: Unlock basic tool recipes and micro-research queue slot 1.
column11: Core no-shards unlock: haul the object home.

column7: Build ST_ANVIL_FORGE and ST_KILN_FURNACE or their prerequisites.
column9: Metalwork/Ceramics T0 tech available; +Engineer MR.
column11: Major infrastructure reward is built by the player.

column7: Create role priority trees and fallback arrays for 4 departments.
column9: FallbackTaskArray enabled; idle-worker warnings reduce.
column11: The colony becomes a system.

column6: First hostile sighting or BaseRisk > threshold.
column7: Build emergency weapon rack, load one MilitiaKit, and drill three civilians.
column9: Civilian DEFEND/HIDE/FLEE simulation unlocked; +MR Security.
column10: Raid response is chaotic if skipped.
column11: Weapons are physical, not spawned.

column6: Scout report or Hunter leader present.
column7: Build cartography board and pin 3 reports from player/scout exploration.
column9: POI rumor system and location placeholders unlocked.
column11: Reward is knowledge, not loot.

column7: Complete any 3 micro-research projects from different families.
column9: MicroResearchStacking enabled and first family cap displayed.
column11: Teaches small additive-feeling but multiplicative-safe upgrades.

column7: Assemble basic generator, storage, and powered station circuit.
column9: PowerQualityFactor telemetry enabled; electronics tier path opens.
column11: No one gives a generator; the camp builds one.

column7: Identify first non-player camp without starting a fight.
column8: Faction camp placeholder selected by worldgen.
column9: Faction diplomacy screen; moral compass comparison.
column10: Unknown faction may expand near player.
column11: Introduces other colonies as simulated peers.

column7: Complete a believable faction trust objective: shared route, prisoner exchange, med aid, or trade convoy.
column9: Access to faction market/services; item rewards only from visible faction stockpile.
column11: Solves OP item problem with logistics credibility.

column7: Find an occupied Remnant satellite site and identify garrison pattern.
column8: Occupied site placeholder: Relay Bastion, Power Tap, Armory Cache, etc.
column9: Unlock site planning board and ControlState telemetry.
column11: Recon mission before dungeon assault.

column7: Clear, infiltrate, bargain, or sabotage a site; extract physical core/module and haul it home.
column9: Major TechNode stalls resolve via ReferenceComponentId / module delivery.
column11: The reward is what the player physically extracts.

column7: Survive first Remnant wake escalation and document trigger cause.
column9: D diagnostic protocol; Research/Engineering leaders gain issue awareness.
column10: Repeated power/noise abuse raises D volatility.
column11: Makes D explainable.

column7: Assemble stacked intel to triangulate antagonist capital region.
column9: Capital search region appears, not exact coordinates.
column11: Prevents instant map marker hand-holding.

column6: Refugees generated by faction collapse/site clear.
column7: Choose accept, refuse, escort elsewhere, or trade custody of refugees.
column8: Wilderness route / player camp / faction camp.
column9: Camp culture shifts; labor or diplomacy outcomes; no binary good/evil loot.
column11: Ties morality to simulation.

column7: Disable or bypass two satellite systems: relay, power tap, recruitment hold, armory cache.
column9: Capital raid difficulty reduced; enemy raid cadence changes.
column11: World state changes the final battle.

column7: Build or claim a high-power beacon array and defend it through startup.
column9: Rescue ending path opens.
column11: Victory requires infrastructure, not one button.

column7: Recover launch/guidance/cryopod reference components from high-tier sites.
column9: Escape ending path opens.
column10: Components are bulk/fragile and attract raids.
column11: No magic ship parts.

column7: Stabilize food, power, defense, morale, and faction treaties for a long-duration colony.
column9: Settlement ending path opens; colony becomes self-sustaining simulation.
column10: Sustained low morale can split colony.
column11: This is survival-state victory.

column6: One ending path reaches final commitment.
column7: Leaders debate evacuation, rescue wait, or settlement; player commits policy.
column9: Locks final objective set and applies camp-wide morale compass result.
column10: Opposed leaders may resign/defect if alignment is terrible.
column11: Final choice is political and human.

column7: Survive final multi-front pressure event: raid, D wake, weather, supply strain.
column9: Complete selected ending.
column11: Tests the whole systemic stack.

column7: Generate persistent outcome report from survivors, factions, losses, leaders, and discovered truth.
column9: New-game modifiers/codex unlocks only; no combat loot handout.
column11: Developer-facing: summary pulled from world state.
